mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals labor market deterioration that reduces corporate earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 in subsequent quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term marginal predictive power is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of positive industrial production growth, supply-side capacity utilization improves corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns during recovery phases, or INDPRO growth shows no significant correlation with 20-day forward returns with p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and consumer spending capacity, which consistently predict positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll revisions coincide with negative 60-day forward returns in greater than 60% of cases, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly inflation accelerates beyond 0.4%, it forces expectations of tighter monetary policy and erodes real corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% threshold, or the inflation signal shows no predictive decay in subsequent quarters.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising unemployment and elevated federal funds rates creates stagflationary pressure that suppresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields positive forward returns in greater than 55% of instances, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant compared to individual series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.0% annualized confirms robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.0% is followed by negative 60-day forward returns in greater than 60% of quarters, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward tests.
